articles.title: Trump Describes His Mind-Set After Shooting at White House Correspondents’ Dinner
articles.summary: Very little was clear about what had happened at the Washington Hilton on Saturday night. But the president wanted to talk about it.

articles.title: The Supreme Court case that could redefine your digital privacy
articles.summary: Police in Virginia used a technique called geofencing to tap into Google's databases to find out who was near the scene of a bank robbery. The Supreme Court will consider whether it is constitutional.

articles.title: For students labeled 'emotionally disturbed,' separation can lead to isolation
articles.summary: Every school has problem students, but some are labeled emotionally disturbed (ED) and taught separately from others.

articles.title: California Man in Custody After Shooting at Dinner Attended by Trump
articles.summary: Shots were fired at the hotel hosting the White House correspondents’ dinner. Authorities said the attack was carried out by a lone gunman who was brought down by the Secret Service.

articles.title: Trump Seeks to Abolish Iran’s Nuclear Stockpile, a Problem He Helped Create
articles.summary: President Trump withdrew from the Obama-era nuclear accord in 2018, saying it was the worst deal ever. But Iran responded with an enrichment spree that haunts the negotiations to this day.

articles.title: A suspect is in custody after Trump is rushed from correspondents' dinner
articles.summary: President Trump and several cabinet members were safely rushed from the event in Washington, D.C. after several loud sounds were heard. The Secret Service said one person was in custody.

articles.title: Trump Cancels Witkoff and Kushner’s Trip for Iran Talks, Saying, ‘We Have All the Cards.’
articles.summary: The last-minute scuttling of the trip to Pakistan was the latest sign of how far apart the two sides are on reaching a deal to end the Iran war.

articles.title: New CEO Steve O'Donnell vows to unite NASCAR and return the fun
articles.summary: Steve O'Donnell was introduced as the sanctioning body's chief executive officer at Talladega Superspeedway on Saturday and vowed to "make some moves" that will return the storied racing series to its

articles.title: In Deep Blue California, a G.O.P.-Backed Voter ID Proposal Makes the Ballot
articles.summary: A Republican-backed initiative has cleared the signature threshold for the November election. Critics say the measure could make it harder for people to vote.

articles.title: Dirk Kempthorne, former Idaho governor and U.S. Interior secretary, dies at 74
articles.summary: Dirk Kempthorne, a Republican, was elected mayor of Boise at age 34 and served seven years before serving one term in the U.S. Senate and then as governor until 2006.

articles.title: In a rare interview, a leader of the world's largest right-wing group talks to NPR
articles.summary: The second-in-command of the RSS, a Hindu nationalist organization in India, rarely speaks to the Western press. Here's what he said about his group's controversial history.

articles.title: Rocky Balboa statue takes up a new home inside Philly art museum
articles.summary: The bronze sculpture is on display inside the Philadelphia Museum of Art as part of a new exhibition on the impact and cultural importance of statues.

articles.title: Roommate charged with two counts of murder in death, disappearance of two USF students
articles.summary: Authorities have filed murder charges against the roommate of a Bangladeshi doctoral student who disappeared with his girlfriend from the University of South Florida.

articles.title: A Year Later, Trump’s ‘Most Exclusive’ Memecoin Event Is a Lot Less Exclusive
articles.summary: Even a Times reporter qualified for the event, which caused outrage last year for providing access to President Trump in exchange for investment in one of his family’s crypto ventures.

articles.title: Texas Can Arrest and Deport People Who Illegally Cross at Mexico Border, Court Says
articles.summary: The court that paused a 2023 law allowing state and local police officers to arrest migrants has now ruled that the measure is legal, a decision likely to be appealed.

articles.title: Mali hit by wave of coordinated attacks from armed groups
articles.summary: Gunfire and explosions have rocked Mali's capital Bamako and other key cities in one of the most significant coordinated attacks in years, as armed groups, including jihadist insurgents and separatist

articles.title: Firm Building Trump’s Ballroom Got a Secret No-Bid Contract for a Nearby Job
articles.summary: The National Park Service increased the value of the contract several times over and then awarded it to Maryland-based Clark Construction, in a process that experts said was highly unusual.

articles.title: Iran's foreign minister leaves Pakistan, then Trump cancels U.S. delegation's travel
articles.summary: After arriving on Friday, Iran's Abbas Araghchi has left Islamabad, prompting President Trump to announce that his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will no longer travel there Saturday for peace

articles.title: Vessels seized by the U.S. this week were part of a global 'shadow fleet'
articles.summary: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Elisabeth Braw, senior fellow at the Atlantic Council, about shadow vessels, after the U.S. military's seizure of two Iranian-linked oil tankers.

articles.title: Kushner and U.S. envoy Witkoff will head to Pakistan for new Iran talks
articles.summary: Iran's foreign minister arrived in Islamabad, and the White House says Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will go there Saturday to try to "move the ball forward towards a deal."

articles.title: Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire. And, Trump eases medical marijuana rules
articles.summary: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their ceasefire for three weeks, President Trump says. And, the Trump administration is easing rules on medical marijuana.

articles.title: Thousands of seafarers stranded by ongoing U.S. blockade on Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: As the U.S. blockade on Iranian ports drags on, thousands of seafarers are stranded on ships, and economic shockwaves ripple around the world.

articles.title: Cuban ambassador says U.S. is to blame for island's crisis
articles.summary: Even as the U.S. threatens military action, a senior Cuban ambassador blames Washington for the country's crisis and says Cuba is prepared for confrontation if diplomacy collapses.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
